content: A commodity supercycle is a prolonged period (10-35 years) of above-trend commodity prices driven by structural demand shifts. Historical supercycles occurred during US industrialization (1890s-1910s), WWII reconstruction (1940s-1950s), and China's urbanization boom (2000s). Supercycles require sustained demand growth from a major economy undergoing rapid development, combined with inelastic supply response.
